item.title: ops/integrations directory and .integration-manifest.json created without security modes — integration config world-listable
item.intent: ops/integrations and .integration-manifest.json must be created with restricted permissions — the manifest reveals which integrations (IMAP, SMTP, CalDAV) are configured on the agent, giving an attacker a precise capability map.
item.description: src/evolve/integrate/manifest.ts saveIntegrationManifest() (lines 35-43): line 38 calls mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MANIFEST_DIR), { recursive: true }) without mode — creates ops/integrations/ with default 0755. Line 39 calls writeFileSync(path, ..., 'utf-8') without mode option — creates .integration-manifest.json with default 0644. Both are wrong. The manifest stores integration state (name, enabled, version) and reveals exactly which integrations (email, calendar, tasks) are wired into the agent. No import from config/defaults.js exists today — needs DIR_MODE_SECRET and FILE_MODE_SECRET added.
acceptanceCriteria: On a multi-user system, a user other than the agent owner cannot list files in ops/integrations/ to discover which integrations (IMAP, SMTP, CalDAV) are configured
.integration-manifest.json is not readable by other users — integration state (name, enabled, version) remains private to the agent owner
